Notice Of Motion To Strike Claims From Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF GREG BAKER Motion To Strike Claims From Cross-Complaint is off calendar because the action is stayed. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion To Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Require Company To Pay All Costs Related To The Action
Off Calendar Nov-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF GREG BAKER Motion To Compel Arbitration is granted pursuant to Appendix A and the court stays this action. The gravamen of plaintiff's action relates to defendant's failure to transfer stock and this action implicates Appendix A. This ruling does not preclude the arbitrator from revisiting the fee/costs issue, especially since the cross-complaint is arbitrable under the Ownership Agreement and that agreement lacks a clause similar to par. 23 in Appendix A. Defendant's merits based arguments, e.g., lack of consideration, violation of Corporations Code sec. 409, time bar, etc. are issues that are not properly before the court at this time. Arbitration clauses are viewed as separable when determining whether they are enforceable. See Rosenthal v. Great W. Fin Sec. Corp., (1996) 14 Cal.4th 394, 416 (arbitrator decides fraud in the inducement claim unless the fraud was directly related to the agreement to arbitrate). Here, the arbitration clause is enforceable and the arbitrator will decide defendant's arguments related to the merits of the action. Defendant fails to establish a waiver of the right to arbitrate. "The law favors arbitration, and waiver will not be 'lightly inferred.' The party claiming the other waived the right to arbitrate 'bears a heavy burden of proof.'" Hoover v. American Income Life Ins. Co. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 1193, 1203. A party seeking to prove waiver of a right to arbitration must demonstrate " '(1) knowledge of an existing right to compel arbitration; (2) acts inconsistent with that existing right; and (3) prejudice to the party opposing arbitration.' " Id. In this case, defendant fails to establish a waiver. The case is in its fledgling stages. Plaintiff has filed a complaint and defendant filed a cross-complaint. No discovery has occurred and defendant fails to show that the machinery of litigation has progressed to such an extent that compelling arbitration at this point would be prejudicial or inequitable to defendant. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-05-2015 Continued To Dec-16-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
